'''Bayley Sironen''' (born 23 December 1996) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}}, {{rlp|hk}}, and {{rlp|lk}} for [[Wynnum Manly Seagulls]] in the [[Queensland Cup]].

He previously played for the [[Wests Tigers]], [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] and [[New Zealand Warriors]] in the [[National Rugby League]].

==Background==
Sironen was born in [[Sydney|Sydney, New South Wales, Australia]]. He is the younger brother of [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] player [[Curtis Sironen]], and son of [[Australia national rugby league team|Australian]] international [[Paul Sironen]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/brothers-curtis-and-bayley-sironen-aim-to-follow-in-father-pauls-footsteps-at-wests-tigers/news-story/eba4980af5fb2f6e4869d62173701fd8|title=Brothers Curtis and Bayley Sironen aim to follow in father Paul's footsteps at Wests Tigers|website=www.dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=25 August 2017}}</ref>

Sironen played his junior rugby league for the [[Holy Cross Rhinos]], before being signed by the [[Wests Tigers]].

==Playing career==
===Early career===
In July 2014, Sironen was named in the [[Australian Schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys]] rugby league squad.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/newslocal/sport/bayley-sironen-has-been-selected-for-the-australian-schoolboys-end-of-year-tour/news-story/f042358a20df0bc3eb8ab888a52b7b88|title=Sironen surname no burden|newspaper=Daily Telegraph|date=22 July 2014|publisher=|access-date=25 August 2017|last1=Connellan|first1=Matthew}}</ref> In 2015 and 2016, he played for the Wests Tigers' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://nycdatabase.org/players/s/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160131072555/http://nycdatabase.org/players/s/|url-status=usurped|archive-date=31 January 2016|title=S|date=27 December 2012|publisher=|access-date=25 August 2017}}</ref> In July 2016, he played for the [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] under-20s team against the [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]] under-20s team.<ref>{{cite web|author=NRL Digital Media |url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2016/07/07/nsw_under_20s_team_a.html |title=NSW Under-20s Team Announcement |publisher=NSWRL |date= 7 July 2016|access-date=2017-07-23}}</ref>

===2017===
In 2017, Sironen graduated to the Tigers' [[Intrust Super Premiership NSW]] team.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2017/02/28/teams_intrust_super_.html|title=TEAMS – Intrust Super Premiership NSW Round 1|first=NRL Digital|last=Media|date=28 February 2017|publisher=|access-date=25 August 2017}}</ref> In round 25 of the [[2017 NRL season]], he made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the Tigers against the [[North Queensland Cowboys]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/updated-team-lists-wests-tigers-v-cowboys/tabid/10874/newsid/111384/default.aspx|title=Updated team lists: Wests Tigers v Cowboys|date=25 August 2017|publisher=|access-date=25 August 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.weststigers.com.au/news/2017/08/25/late_changes_nrl_rou.af_news.html|title=Late Changes: NRL Round 25 vs. Cowboys|first=NRL Digital|last=Media|publisher=|access-date=25 August 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170825145419/http://www.weststigers.com.au/news/2017/08/25/late_changes_nrl_rou.af_news.html|archive-date=25 August 2017|url-status=dead}}</ref> He was contracted to the Tigers until the end of 2018.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.theaustralian.com.au/sport/nrl/bayley-sironen-to-make-wests-tigers-debut-against-cowboys/news-story/048b30628fc57d8f458ba7afc4f47733|title=Bayley Sironen to make Wests Tigers debut against Cowboys|website=www.theaustralian.com.au|access-date=25 August 2017}}</ref>

===2018===
In 2018, Sironen was selected to play for the [[New South Wales Residents rugby league team|New South Wales Residents]] against the [[Queensland Residents rugby league team|Queensland Residents]] side.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2018/06/24/final-team--intrust-super-nsw-residents/|title=FINAL TEAM – Intrust Super NSW Residents|date=24 June 2018|publisher=|access-date=4 July 2018}}</ref>
On September 5, Sironen signed a one-year deal to join South Sydney for the 2019 season after failing to secure a first grade spot with Wests.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rabbitohs.com.au/news/2018/09/05/rabbitohs-sign-kurt-dillon-and-bayley-sironen/|title = Rabbitohs sign Kurt Dillon and Bayley Sironen|date = 5 September 2018}}</ref>

===2019===
Sironen made his debut for an [[State of Origin series|Origin]] depleted South Sydney side (first grade player #1151)<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.ssralmanac.com/mobile/players_s/player_sironen_bayley.htm|title = South Sydney Rabbitohs Rugby League Player Report – Bayley Sironen}}</ref> in round 12 of the [[2019 NRL season]] against Parramatta which ended in a 14–26 defeat at the new [[Western Sydney Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2019-05-31/nrl-parramatta-eels-beat-south-sydney-rabbitohs/11165196/|title=Parramatta Eels beat South Sydney Rabbitohs 26–14 in NRL round 12|newspaper=ABC News|date=31 May 2019}}</ref>

===2020===
In round 7 of the [[2020 NRL season]], Sironen scored his first try in the top grade as South Sydney were defeated by Penrith 20–12 at [[Bankwest Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/andrew-johns-issues-reality-check-to-south-sydney/072f83c8-51e0-48a0-979b-efa28bcfb553/|title=Andrew Johns issues reality check to South Sydney|website=wwos.nine.com.au}}</ref>

Sironen played 19 games throughout the season including South Sydney's preliminary final defeat against Penrith.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2020-10-17/nrl-preliminary-final-penrith-panthers-south-sydney-scorecentre/12774550/|title=Penrith Panthers beat South Sydney Rabbitohs 20–16 to set up NRL grand final against Melbourne Storm|newspaper=ABC News|date=17 October 2020}}</ref>

On 20 November, he signed a three-year contract with [[New Zealand Warriors|New Zealand]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2020-transfer-centre-james-roberts-wests-tigers-contract-south-sydney-rabbitohs/news-story/0163e57177a9d21cae3f15ac25d378ca?fbclid=IwAR2ULy53_6GfeUiYheThpygBip908XDjXNzxj-KUV7DYBGuy9PINiXcEN3I/|title=Rabbitohs release gun to Warriors, Johns signs with Knights|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=20 November 2020}}</ref>

===2021===
In round 1 of the [[2021 NRL season]], he scored a try on debut for New Zealand in a 19–6 victory over the Gold Coast.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-03-13/nrl-sydney-roosters-penrith-score-big-first-round-wins/13243368/|title=Sydney Roosters break first-round record with 46–4 win over Manly, Penrith Panthers thump Cowboys 46–4|newspaper=ABC News|date=13 March 2021}}</ref>
Sironen played 19 games for New Zealand in the [[2021 NRL season]] where the club finished 12th on the table and missed the finals.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/nrl-2021-rugby-league-finals-series-dragons-raiders-tigers-bulldogs/vkf5nnzrtnwz1r06hrfmfw8oe/|title=NRL 2021: Why every bottom-eight side missed the finals|website=www.sportingnews.com}}</ref>

===2022===
Sironen made a total of 13 appearances for the New Zealand Warriors in the [[2022 NRL season]] as they finished 15th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/warriors/warriors-farewell-15-players-after-return-to-new-zealand/news-story/10c5db674841c74a716fce5ece20e047/|title=Warriors farewell 15 players after return to New Zealand|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>

===2023===
On 5 October 2023, it was reported that he had signed with the [[Catalans Dragons]] ahead of the 2024 season on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=5 Oct 2023|accessdate=8 Oct 2023|title=NRL forward Bayley Sironen makes Super League move after leaving New Zealand Warriors|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/nrl-forward-bayley-sironen-makes-super-league-catalans-dragons-move}}</ref>

===2026===
On 18 February 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Wynnum Manly Seagulls]] in the [[Queensland Cup]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=18 Feb 2026|author=Callum Walker|title=Ex-Catalans Dragons forward Bayley Sironen finds new club|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-catalans-dragons-forward-bayley-sironen-finds-new-club/}}</ref>
